summary: Frontend-only Playwright testing of Faisal Saifi portfolio SPA. Verified navbar, smooth-scroll, Work tab filtering, Pricing CTA toast + window.open, Contact form (empty error + success toast), mobile slide-in menu, hero/footer/cursor presence. Identified one functional issue: Work lightbox close button does not dismiss the lightbox.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking the lightbox-close (X) button does NOT dismiss the lightbox. Programmatic click via Playwright (force=True) on [data-testid="lightbox-close"] left the lightbox visible (confirmed via screenshot showing the OPD Growth Campaign lightbox still on screen while pricing toast fired underneath). Suspect cause: the close button is positioned via `absolute top-6 right-6` OUTSIDE the inner card. Clicking it bubbles up to the parent backdrop, but the backdrop's onClick fires setLightbox(null) — however since the button is rendered as a direct child of the same backdrop, and the icon inside (lucide X) may be receiving pointer events first; OR an outer overlay/cursor element is intercepting. Recommended fix: add `e.stopPropagation()` is not needed since both call setLightbox(null); however ensure the Cursor component (z higher?) is not blocking pointer events — verify the cursor-dot/ring have `pointer-events:none` in index.css.

action_items: Fix Work lightbox close: verify Cursor (.cursor-dot/.cursor-ring) elements have `pointer-events: none` in index.css; if already set, ensure the close <button> is not being overlapped by another fixed-z element. Consider raising lightbox-close z-index above the lightbox content and/or attaching the close handler to a wrapping div.

Optional: include the close button's onClick with explicit e.stopPropagation() to make intent clear (current behaviour relies on backdrop onClick fallback which also calls setLightbox(null), but the visible result shows it isn't firing).
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar: nav-logo button calls go('hero') but there is no element with id='hero' (Hero section likely uses a different id). Smooth-scroll on logo click silently no-ops. Verify Hero.jsx exposes id='hero'.

Contact.jsx: window.open is called unconditionally even when popup blockers may suppress it; consider using an <a target='_blank'> approach OR check the return value of window.open and fallback to location.href.
Cursor component: ensure cursor-dot/cursor-ring elements have `pointer-events: none` in CSS — they are fixed-position and could intercept clicks like the lightbox close X.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified (PASS): site-navbar renders, scrolled state adds `backdrop-blur-xl`, all 6 nav-link-* and nav-hire-me-btn present, hero-view-work-btn + hero-download-cv-btn present, smooth-scroll to #services works, Work tabs: All=9 cards, Video=2 cards (filter works), lightbox OPENS on work-card click, pricing-popular-badge present, pricing-cta-growth shows 'Opening WhatsApp — talk to you soon!' toast and triggers window.open(wa.me/918307399295...), Contact empty-submit shows 'Please fill in your name, contact and a short message.' toast, filled Contact submit shows success toast + opens WhatsApp URL, mobile-menu-toggle opens slide-in panel with mobile-nav-link-* entries which close menu and scroll on click. FAIL: lightbox-close button does not dismiss the lightbox (confirmed visually). Other elements present: cursor-dot/cursor-ring, footer. Did NOT verify download-cv behaviour, footer social icon links, contact card click toasts individually, scroll-reveal class additions.
rca of the issue: Lightbox: button onClick={() => setLightbox(null)} appears correct in source. Likely cause is a higher-z overlay (Cursor elements at z near top, or sonner Toaster) intercepting the click on the absolutely positioned close button at top-6/right-6. Reproduction: open https://685e6994-e5c8-44f7-b7c4-72d960f8359e.preview.emergentagent.com → scroll to Work → click any card → click X button at top-right of lightbox → lightbox remains visible. Mitigation: add `pointer-events: none` to .cursor-dot/.cursor-ring and/or raise lightbox container z-index and ensure close button has z above any overlays.
